introduction:
The Ration Card(Rice card) in Andhra Pradesh is issued by the Department of Consumer Affairs, Food & Civil Supplies, Government of Andhra Pradesh. It entitles eligible families to receive subsidised food grains — rice, wheat, sugar, and kerosene — through the Public Distribution System (PDS) at their designated Fair Price Shop (FPS).

The ration card serves as a crucial identity and address proof accepted across government services. Andhra Pradesh distributes ration cards under the National Food Security Act (NFSA) as well as state-specific schemes like Anna Canteen and NTR Bharosa Pension Scheme.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Applying for a new ration card is FREE of cost
  • Processing time is approximately 30 working days
  • You must be a permanent resident of Andhra Pradesh to apply
  • Only one ration card per family is permitted
  • Aadhaar linking is mandatory for all family members
  • Track applications at spb.ap.gov.in or ap.meeseva.gov.in
  • Helpline: 1800-425-2977 (Toll-Free)
documentsRequired:
Documents Required for Ration Card

Note: Submit self-attested photocopies at the office and carry original documents for verification by the officer.
Identity Proof (Any one)
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Voter ID Card
  • PAN Card
  • Passport
  • Driving Licence
  • MNREGA Job Card
Address Proof (Any one)
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Electricity Bill (recent)
  • Water / Telephone Bill
  • Bank Passbook
  • Registered Rent Agreement
  • Property Tax Receipt
Income & Family Documents
  • Income Certificate (from MRO / Tahsildar)
  • Birth Certificates of children
  • Marriage Certificate (if applicable)
  • Passport-size photos of all family members
Category Guide
  • AAY (Priority — White): Poorest of poor families
  • PHH (Priority Household): NFSA beneficiaries
  • NPHH (Non-Priority): Above poverty line families

Note: BPL/PHH category requires income certificate from MRO.
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via Meeseva / AP Food Portal

Andhra Pradesh offers online ration card applications through Meeseva (citizen service centers) and the AP Food Department portal. You need an Aadhaar-linked mobile number.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit the Official Portal
    Go to ap.meeseva.gov.in or spb.ap.gov.in
  2. Register / Login
    Click "New Registration". Enter your Aadhaar number, mobile number, and verify with OTP sent to your registered mobile.
  3. Select Service
    Go to Civil Supplies → "New Ration Card Application"
  4. Fill Application Form
    Enter all required details:
    • Head of family name (as per Aadhaar)
    • All family members with Aadhaar numbers
    • Select ration card category (PHH / NPHH / AAY)
    • Select your village / ward and nearest FPS
  5. Upload Documents
    Upload scanned copies of identity proof, address proof, income certificate, and family photos. File size limit: 200 KB per file (JPG/PDF)
  6. Pay Service Fee & Submit
    A nominal Meeseva service charge (~₹35) applies. Submit and note your Application Reference Number from the SMS received.
  7. Verification by Revenue Inspector (RI)
    An RI / VRO will visit your home within 15 working days for verification.
Tip: Keep all original documents ready at home on the day of RI verification.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply Offline at Meeseva Center / MRO Office (Direct Visit)

You can visit any Meeseva Center or the local Mandal Revenue Office (MRO) to apply without internet access.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit Meeseva Center or MRO Office
    Working hours: Monday – Saturday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM (closed on 2nd Saturday & public holidays)
  2. Collect Application Form
    Obtain the Ration Card Application Form from the Meeseva operator or MRO counter. Service charge of approximately ₹35 applies at Meeseva centers.
  3. Fill the Form
    Fill in black ink / BLOCK LETTERS with:
    • Head of family name (as per Aadhaar)
    • All family member names & Aadhaar numbers
    • Complete residential address
    • Category (PHH / NPHH / AAY) & FPS details
    • Active mobile number
  4. Submit Documents & Get Receipt
    Submit form + self-attested photocopies. Carry originals for verification. You receive an Acknowledgement Receipt with application number.
  5. Home Verification by Revenue Inspector
    A Revenue Inspector (RI) visits your home within 15 working days to verify details.
  6. Collect Ration Card
    After approval, collect your ration card from the Meeseva center or MRO office within 30 working days. SMS notification will be sent to your mobile.
Processing Time: Approximately 30 working days from date of application.
afterApplying:
After Applying — What Next?

Track Your Application

Visit spb.ap.gov.in or ap.meeseva.gov.in
Enter your Application Reference Number to check status.

Download Digital Ration Card

Once approved, download your ration card from the AP Food portal or Meeseva portal. The digital copy is fully valid at Fair Price Shops.

Link Aadhaar to Ration Card

Mandatory for all family members. Visit your nearest FPS or Meeseva center to complete Aadhaar seeding for uninterrupted grain entitlement.

Raise a Grievance

If not processed within 30 days, call Toll-Free: 1800-425-2977 or visit spb.ap.gov.in to file an online complaint.
